evaluation.summary: The variance-to-mean ratio R(k) for primorial gaps shows a power-law scaling with exponent about 2.19, slightly better than logarithmic fits for k up to 15.
next_directions: Extend the analysis to larger primorial indices (k>15) to assess scaling stability.
Conduct statistical hypothesis testing to confirm the power-law exponent versus alternative models.
Investigate potential boundary or truncation effects in the primorial gap computation.
